questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The simple interest on $5000$ at $8\%$ per annum for $3$ years is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{SI}=\dfrac{P\,r\,t}{100}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{5000\times 8\times 3}{100}=1200$$

**Answer.** $1200$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — Time, Speed and Distance
questions.stem: A car travels $180$ km in $3$ hours. Its average speed is _____ km/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Speed = distance / time.

**Working.** $\dfrac{180}{3}=60$ km/h.

**Answer.** $60$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The probability of getting a head in a single toss of a fair coin is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probability = favourable / total equally likely outcomes.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{2}=0.5$.

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: DROUGHT : WATER :: FAMINE : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'crisis : the resource that is scarce'.

**Working.** A drought is a severe shortage of water; a famine is a severe shortage of food.

**Answer.** food.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded three times (into one-eighth) and one hole is punched through all layers. When unfolded, the number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded three times into an eighth with one hole punched.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='30' y='25' width='30' height='60' fill='rgba(244,63,94,0.10)' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='55' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Three folds create $2^3 = 8$ layers.

**Working.** One punch through $8$ layers gives $8$ holes.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of positive divisors of $36$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $N=p^a q^b$, the divisor count is $(a+1)(b+1)$.

**Working.** $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$, so $(2+1)(2+1)=9$.

**Answer.** $9$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express the right side as a power of the same base.

**Working.** $32=2^5$, so $x=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Under colour-coded biomedical-waste segregation, anatomical/infected waste for incineration is typically placed in the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Yellow = incinerable anatomical/soiled waste; red = contaminated recyclable plastics; blue/white = glass/sharps.

**Working.**
Yellow bag.

**Answer.** Yellow

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $10$ identical machines each produce $75\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$75+10\log_{10}10=85.$$

**Answer.** $85\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">8</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$8+1.5=9.5.$$

**Answer.** $9.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Waste of mass $1000\,\text{kg}$ at loose density $100\,\text{kg/m}^3$ is compacted to $500\,\text{kg/m}^3$. The volume reduction is _____ %.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $VR=\left(1-\dfrac{V_2}{V_1}\right)\times100=\left(1-\dfrac{\rho_1}{\rho_2}\right)\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{500}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The main purpose of maintaining ~850–1100 °C with adequate residence time in an MSW incinerator is to
options: ensure complete combustion and destroy dioxins/pathogens
reduce the need for air pollution control
questions.solution:
**Concept.** High temperature + 2 s residence ensures complete burnout and dioxin destruction.

**Working.**
Complete combustion.

**Answer.** Complete combustion

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $2$ identical sources each $75\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$75+10\log2=78.01.$$

**Answer.** $78.01\,\text{dB}$

questions.solution:

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $2000\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$2000(1-60/100)=800.$$

**Answer.** $800\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="126" width="50" height="24" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="122" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">500</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{500}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste of $1000\,\text{kg}$ yields landfill gas at $0.15\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$. The total gas generated is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas $=$ mass $\times$ specific yield.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.15=150.$$

**Answer.** $150\,\text{m}^3$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 5 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 500 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{5\times1000}{500}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=120\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 25 m,  σ_z = 25 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120}{\pi\times6\times25\times25}e^{-50^2/(2\times25^2)}=0.00137851.$$

**Answer.** $13.785\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=3.75\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $3.75\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">5</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">0.5</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$100+5\times28+0.5\times265=372.5.$$

**Answer.** $372.5$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">20</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="78" width="50" height="72" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="74" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">12</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$20\times\dfrac{50}{20}+12\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=99.4.$$

**Answer.** $99.4$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics. Reading $\mu_{max}$ (plateau) and the half-saturation constant $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Monod: $\mu=\dfrac{\mu_{max}S}{K_s+S}$, with $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times100}{50+100}=0.333.$$

**Answer.** $0.333\,\text{h}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $8$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="100" width="60" height="50" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 100</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$100e^{-0.1\times8}=44.93.$$

**Answer.** $44.93$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=45\%$, $H=7\%$, $O=40\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 18</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(45)+1419(7-40/8)=18003.$$

**Answer.** $18003\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $50\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:

**Working.**
$$1000(1-50/100)=500.$$

**Answer.** $500\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $20\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">10</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times10}{20}=0.025.$$

**Answer.** $0.025\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste $2000\,\text{kg}$ yields $0.1\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$ gas. Total gas is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas = mass × yield.

**Working.**
$$2000\times0.1=200.$$

**Answer.** $200$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $4\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times200/4})\times100=99.75\%.$$

**Answer.** $99.75\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Select all statutes/instruments that are central environmental legislation in India.
options: The Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
The Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The Water Act (1974), Air Act (1981) and EP Act (1986) are the core environmental statutes; the Companies Act is corporate law.

**Working.**
First three are environmental law.

**Answer.** Water, Air and EP Acts

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $20\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">25</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times25}{20}=0.0625.$$

**Answer.** $0.0625\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: In the pH range 8.3–9.5 of most natural waters, alkalinity is predominantly due to

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="160" x2="305" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="14" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="240" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant added →</text><line x1="38" y1="89" x2="305" y2="89" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/><text x="42" y="86" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">pH 7</text><path d="M38 150 C120 146 160 140 168 89 C176 38 230 32 305 28" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="168" cy="89" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence pt</text></svg>
figure.caption: Alkalinity end-points (phenolphthalein, methyl-orange) on a titration curve.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Between pH ~6.3 and 10.3 the carbonate system is dominated by $\text{HCO}_3^-$.

**Working.**
Bicarbonate dominates.

**Answer.** Bicarbonate

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: A landfill has a usable air-space of $5,00,000\,\text{m}^3$ and receives compacted waste at $50,000\,\text{m}^3/\text{yr}$. Its design life is _____ years.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life $=\dfrac{\text{capacity}}{\text{annual fill}}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{500000}{50000}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="123" width="50" height="27" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="119" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">18</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80\times\dfrac{50}{20}+18\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=274.1.$$

**Answer.** $274.1$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.08\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $6$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.08\times6}=1616.$$

**Answer.** $1616$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 6</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{6}}=75620.$$

**Answer.** $₹75620$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.05\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $9$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 2000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$2000e^{0.05\times9}=3137.$$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=6\%$, $O=42\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 14.5</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(6-42/8)=14544.$$

**Answer.** $14544\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $100\,\text{g/s}$ of a pollutant. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level concentration on the plume centreline (full ground reflection, $y=0$) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 5 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 20 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $C=\dfrac{Q}{\pi u\sigma_y\sigma_z}\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{H^2}{2\sigma_z^2}\right)$ (ground, centreline, full reflection); $H,u,\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times5\times30\times20}e^{-50^2/(2\times20^2)}=0.00046619\,\text{g/m}^3.$$

**Answer.** $4.662\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 150 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times150/6})\times100=95.02\%.$$

**Answer.** $95.02\%$
